conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your situation, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the first one:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child - my daughter Alex. She's 8 years old and lives with me here at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

**Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with her and are you responsible for things like her food, clothing, and general care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. She lives with me and I'm responsible for all her care - her food, clothing, everything she needs day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Let me ask the next question:

**Is Alex in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?** (This would typically apply to children aged 16-19, but I want to confirm her current status since she's 8 years old - is she attending school?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh yes, she's in primary school - she's only 8, so she's in Year 4 at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions:

**Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for Alex, or does anyone else have an equal or greater responsibility for her care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her. I'm the one who takes care of her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ **Alex meets the age requirement** - She is 8 years old (under 16)
✓ **You are responsible for her** - She lives with you and you're responsible for her food, clothing, and general care
✓ **You live in the UK** - This satisfies the residency requirement
✓ **No competing claims** - Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the key eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can apply for Child Benefit for Alex, and you should receive payments to help with the costs of raising her.

Please note: If you or your partner have individual income over £50,000, you may need to pay a tax charge (called the High Income Child Benefit Charge), but you can still claim and receive Child Benefit.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: 1) She is 8 years old, which is under 16 years old, meeting the age requirement. 2) She lives with the claimant who is responsible for her care, including food, clothing, and general needs. 3) She attends primary school (Year 4). 4) The claimant lives in the UK. 5) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and no one else has equal or greater responsibility for her care. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their daughter Alex. The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex who is 8 years old and lives with them, attends school, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her. All the key eligibility criteria are satisfied.
